title: Building a Page Structure
objectives: Understand the purpose of HTML as the skeleton of every web page
Use semantic tags (html, head, body, header, main, footer) to organize content
Create a valid HTML5 document from scratch
hints: Every HTML page starts with <!DOCTYPE html> — this tells the browser which version of HTML you're using
The <head> contains metadata (title, charset) while <body> contains everything the user sees
Semantic tags like <header>, <main>, and <footer> tell browsers and screen readers what each section means
assessmentCriteria: Page has a valid DOCTYPE declaration
Document contains html, head, and body elements
Page uses semantic elements: header, main, and footer
Head includes a title and charset meta tag
